edges.description: Persistent viral antigens drive chronic immune activation and cytokine perturbations.
Sustained antigen exposure from viral reservoirs leads to CD8+ T cell exhaustion and CD4+ tissue trafficking.
edges.target: T Cell Exhaustion and Tissue Trafficking

edges.description: Viral persistence and type I interferon-driven inflammation reduce serotonin through diminished tryptophan absorption, platelet hyperactivation, and enhanced MAO turnover.
Persistent viral antigens trigger mast cell degranulation and release of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Reactivated latent viruses may amplify chronic inflammatory signaling and humoral immune activation.
Chronic herpesvirus antigen stimulation contributes to CD8+ T cell exhaustion dynamics in PASC.
edges.target: T Cell Exhaustion and Tissue Trafficking

edges.description: EBV reactivation serology is independently associated with the post-viral fatigue domain of Long COVID.
Systemic inflammatory cytokines penetrate the blood-brain barrier and activate microglia.
Chronic immune activation disrupts mitochondrial function and oxidative phosphorylation.
Immune dysregulation and elevated cytokines promote mast cell activation syndrome.
Systemic inflammation and immune-mediated damage disrupt autonomic nervous system function.
Inflammatory cytokines cause widespread muscle pain.
Inflammatory cytokines drive joint pain without overt inflammation.
Immune dysregulation and reduced CD8+ T cell surveillance permit latent herpesvirus (EBV, HHV-6) reactivation in B-cell reservoirs.
Microclot-mediated capillary blockage impairs oxygen delivery, reducing mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation.
Impaired microcirculatory oxygen exchange contributes to persistent fatigue.
Reduced pulmonary capillary perfusion and oxygen delivery contribute to breathlessness.
Peripheral serotonin reduction impedes vagus nerve activity and impairs hippocampal signaling.
Serotonin modulates autonomic tone; its depletion contributes to dysautonomia.
Reduced serotonin levels contribute to depressed mood through classical monoamine mechanisms.
Impaired vagal anti-inflammatory reflex permits unchecked neuroinflammation.
Disrupted hippocampal signaling impairs memory formation and cognitive function.
Autonomic dysfunction directly causes excessive heart rate increase upon standing.
Dysautonomia produces awareness of abnormal heartbeat patterns.
Impaired oxidative phosphorylation forces a compensatory shift toward glycolysis with resulting oxidative stress.
Reduced cellular ATP production underlies persistent fatigue.
Insufficient mitochondrial energy reserve causes symptom exacerbation after exertion.
Oxidative stress damages mitochondrial components, creating a self-reinforcing feedback loop of energy deficit.
Histamine release from mast cells drives autonomic dysfunction and POTS symptoms.
Mast cell mediators cross the blood-brain barrier and activate neuroinflammatory cascades.
Mast cell-derived histamine directly increases heart rate.
Mast cell degranulation in the gastrointestinal tract causes diarrhea.
Gastrointestinal mast cell activation produces nausea.
Microglial activation and inflammatory cytokines impair learning, memory, and executive function.
Neuroinflammatory mediators sensitize pain pathways and drive chronic headache.
Inflammatory cytokines in the brain disrupt monoamine signaling and promote depressed mood.
Neuroinflammation in limbic circuitry amplifies anxiety and emotional dysregulation.
Increased BBB permeability permits CNS entry of systemic inflammatory cytokines and immune cells, amplifying neuroinflammatory activation.
BBB permeability-mediated CNS access of inflammatory mediators disrupts synaptic plasticity and cognitive function.
Gut dysbiosis modulates systemic inflammation through the gut-brain axis, promoting neuroinflammatory cascades.
Gut dysbiosis disrupts immune homeostasis and exacerbates systemic inflammation.
Autoantibodies drive immune complex-mediated inflammation and sustain chronic immune activation.
Anti-autonomic receptor autoantibodies may contribute to dysautonomia symptoms.
Episodes of post-exertional malaise exacerbate and prolong baseline fatigue.
Sleep disruption impairs memory consolidation and executive function.
Chronic insomnia is a significant predictor for the onset of anxiety.
Persistent insomnia is a significant risk factor for the development of depression.

edges.source: Ivabradine therapy for post-COVID POTS-like tachycardia
Ivabradine therapy for post-COVID POTS-like tachycardia
Beta adrenergic agent therapy for POTS-associated symptoms
Beta adrenergic agent therapy for POTS-associated symptoms

nodes.description: Structured energy-envelope management to reduce post-exertional symptom exacerbation.

evidence.reference_title: Practical Recommendations for Exercise Training in Patients with Long COVID with or without Post-exertional Malaise: A Best Practice Proposal.
evidence.snippet: clinical experience has shown that the presence of post-exertional malaise (PEM) is a significant barrier to physical exercise training in people with long COVID.

evidence.reference_title: ME/CFS and Long COVID share similar symptoms and biological abnormalities: road map to the literature.
evidence.snippet: The illness (Long COVID) is similar to myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS)

nodes.description: H1 and H2 receptor antagonists (such as fexofenadine and famotidine) to address mast cell activation symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Antihistamines improve cardiovascular manifestations and other symptoms of long-COVID attributed to mast cell activation.
evidence.snippet: Patients were treated with fexofenadine (180 mg/day) and famotidine (40 mg/day). Fatigue, brain fog, abdominal disorders, and increased heart rate were evaluated in treated and untreated patients at baseline and 20 days later.

evidence.reference_title: Antihistamines improve cardiovascular manifestations and other symptoms of long-COVID attributed to mast cell activation.
evidence.snippet: Long-COVID symptoms disappeared completely in 29% of treated patients. There was a significant improvement in each of the considered symptoms

nodes.description: Increased anxiety levels and panic symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: The global prevalence of depression, anxiety, and sleep disorder among patients coping with Post COVID-19 syndrome (long COVID): a systematic review and meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet: The pooled prevalence of depression and anxiety among patients coping with Post COVID-19 syndrome was estimated to be 23% (95% CI: 20%-26%; I2 = 99.9%) based on data from 143 studies with 7,782,124 participants and 132 studies with 9,320,687 participants, respectively.

evidence.reference_title: Anxiety and depression symptoms among patients with long COVID: a retrospective cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Patients suffering from post-acute sequelae of COVID-19 (PASC) have a higher prevalence of anxiety and depression than the general population.

nodes.description: Joint pain without inflammation.

evidence.reference_title: ME/CFS and Long COVID share similar symptoms and biological abnormalities: road map to the literature.
evidence.snippet: They develop persistent fatigue, cognitive problems, headaches, disrupted sleep, myalgias and arthralgias

nodes.description: SARS-CoV-2 infection can trigger preexisting and de novo production of autoantibodies through B-cell dysregulation, molecular mimicry, and bystander activation. These autoantibodies may contribute to the persistence and diversity of Long COVID symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Autoimmunity in long COVID.
evidence.snippet: Several studies show preexisting and/or de novo production of autoantibodies after infection with SARS-CoV-2, but the persistence of these antibodies and their role in causing long COVID is debated.

evidence.reference_title: Diverse immunological dysregulation, chronic inflammation, and impaired erythropoiesis in long COVID patients with chronic fatigue syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Serology revealed an elevation in a variety of autoantibodies in LC.

nodes.description: Dysautonomia, particularly postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome (POTS), develops in many Long COVID patients. This may be related to virus- or immune-mediated disruption of the autonomic nervous system resulting in orthostatic intolerance syndromes.

evidence.reference_title: Autonomic dysfunction in 'long COVID': rationale, physiology and management strategies.
evidence.snippet: we posit that this condition may be related to a virus- or immune-mediated disruption of the autonomic nervous system resulting in orthostatic intolerance syndromes.

evidence.reference_title: Autonomic dysfunction in 'long COVID': rationale, physiology and management strategies.
evidence.snippet: 'Post-acute COVID' (known colloquially as 'long COVID') is emerging as a prevalent syndrome. It encompasses a plethora of debilitating symptoms (including breathlessness, chest pain, palpitations and orthostatic intolerance)

nodes.description: Class-level beta-blocker therapy to reduce orthostatic tachycardia and palpitations in Long-COVID dysautonomia phenotypes.
nodes.id: Beta adrenergic agent therapy for POTS-associated symptoms

evidence.reference_title: Oral medications for the treatment of postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome; a systematic review of studies before and during the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence.snippet: Ivabradine, midodrine, and beta-blockers currently appear to be reasonable front-line choices in pharmacologic management of POTS (PASC associated and otherwise).

nodes.description: Long COVID is associated with increased blood-brain barrier (BBB) permeability resulting from endothelial dysfunction and tight junction disruption. Increased BBB permeability permits systemic inflammatory cytokines and viral antigens to access the brain parenchyma, driving neuroinflammation and cognitive symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Vagus nerve SARS-CoV-2 infection and inflammatory reflex dysfunction: Is there a causal relationship?
evidence.snippet: The vagus nerve inflammatory reflex is an important, well-characterized mechanism for the reflexive suppression of cytokine storm, and its experimental or clinical impairment facilitates the onset and progression of hyperinflammation.

evidence.reference_title: Mast cell activation triggered by SARS-CoV-2 causes inflammation in brain microvascular endothelial cells and microglia.
evidence.snippet: MC activation and degranulation destroyed the tight junction proteins in brain microvascular endothelial cells and induced the activation and proliferation of microglia.

nodes.description: Brain fog, difficulty concentrating, memory problems, and reduced executive function. Often referred to as cognitive dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: ME/CFS and Long COVID share similar symptoms and biological abnormalities: road map to the literature.
evidence.snippet: They develop persistent fatigue, cognitive problems, headaches, disrupted sleep

evidence.reference_title: Serotonin reduction in post-acute sequelae of viral infection.
evidence.snippet: Peripheral serotonin reduction, in turn, impedes the activity of the vagus nerve and thereby impairs hippocampal responses and memory.

nodes.description: Depressed mood and emotional dysregulation.

evidence.reference_title: The global prevalence of depression, anxiety, and sleep disorder among patients coping with Post COVID-19 syndrome (long COVID): a systematic review and meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet: The pooled prevalence of depression and anxiety among patients coping with Post COVID-19 syndrome was estimated to be 23% (95% CI: 20%-26%; I2 = 99.9%) based on data from 143 studies with 7,782,124 participants

evidence.reference_title: Anxiety and depression symptoms among patients with long COVID: a retrospective cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Overall, patients with PASC may have experienced a heavier burden of newly manifest anxiety and depression symptoms compared to patients seen in the GIM clinic.

nodes.description: Loose or watery bowel movements persisting after acute infection.

evidence.reference_title: Characterizing Long COVID: Deep Phenotype of a Complex Condition.
evidence.snippet: curated 59 relevant manuscripts that described clinical manifestations in 81 cohorts three weeks or more following acute COVID-19, and mapped 287 unique clinical findings to HPO terms

nodes.description: Shortness of breath and breathing difficulties persisting after acute infection.

evidence.reference_title: Autonomic dysfunction in 'long COVID': rationale, physiology and management strategies.
evidence.snippet: It encompasses a plethora of debilitating symptoms (including breathlessness, chest pain, palpitations and orthostatic intolerance)

nodes.description: Persistent endothelial damage and formation of amyloid-like fibrin microclots impair microcirculation. These anomalous fibrin deposits resist normal fibrinolysis and can block capillaries, limiting oxygen delivery to tissues and potentially explaining the diverse symptomatology of Long COVID.

evidence.reference_title: A central role for amyloid fibrin microclots in long COVID/PASC: origins and therapeutic implications.
evidence.snippet: The result, as is strongly manifested in platelet-poor plasma (PPP) of individuals with Long COVID, is extensive fibrin amyloid microclots that can persist, can entrap other proteins, and that may lead to the production of various autoantibodies.

evidence.reference_title: A central role for amyloid fibrin microclots in long COVID/PASC: origins and therapeutic implications.
evidence.snippet: the ability of these fibrin amyloid microclots (fibrinaloids) to block up capillaries, and thus to limit the passage of red blood cells and hence O2 exchange, can actually underpin the majority of these symptoms

nodes.description: Persistent, debilitating fatigue that is not relieved by rest.

evidence.reference_title: ME/CFS and Long COVID share similar symptoms and biological abnormalities: road map to the literature.
evidence.snippet: They develop persistent fatigue, cognitive problems, headaches, disrupted sleep, myalgias and arthralgias, post-exertional malaise, orthostatic intolerance and other symptoms that greatly interfere with their ability to function

nodes.description: Persistent gut microbiota dysbiosis has been observed in Long COVID patients, with alterations in microbial composition linked to immune inflammation and neurological manifestations through gut-lung and gut-brain axis disruption.

evidence.reference_title: Gut and airway microbiota dysbiosis and their role in COVID-19 and long-COVID.
evidence.snippet: the possible persistence of gut dysbiosis after disease resolution may be linked to long-COVID syndrome and particularly to its neurological manifestations

evidence.reference_title: Gut and airway microbiota dysbiosis and their role in COVID-19 and long-COVID.
evidence.snippet: Preliminary data seem to suggest that gut and airway dysbiosis might play a role in COVID-19 and in long-COVID neurological symptoms.

nodes.description: Chronic or recurring headaches.

evidence.reference_title: ME/CFS and Long COVID share similar symptoms and biological abnormalities: road map to the literature.
evidence.snippet: They develop persistent fatigue, cognitive problems, headaches, disrupted sleep

nodes.description: Adjunctive therapy under pressure-controlled oxygen conditions, studied for fatigue, cognitive, neuropsychiatric, and cardiopulmonary Long-COVID symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy on Long COVID Symptoms: A Breath of Fresh Air.
evidence.snippet: They suggested that HBOT can improve quality of life, fatigue, cognition, neuropsychiatric symptoms and cardiopulmonary functions.

evidence.reference_title: Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy on Long COVID Symptoms: A Breath of Fresh Air.
evidence.snippet: HBOT is a safe treatment and has shown some benefits for long COVID symptoms.

nodes.description: Long COVID is associated with reduced mitochondrial ATP production and impaired oxidative phosphorylation, contributing to fatigue, cognitive disturbances, and exercise intolerance.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial dysfunction in long COVID: mechanisms, consequences, and potential therapeutic approaches.
evidence.snippet: Emerging evidence has pointed to mitochondrial dysfunction as a potential underpinning mechanism contributing to the persistence and diversity of long COVID symptoms.

nodes.description: Personalized exercise-based physical therapy with intensity adjusted to PEM severity and tolerance.

evidence.reference_title: Practical Recommendations for Exercise Training in Patients with Long COVID with or without Post-exertional Malaise: A Best Practice Proposal.
evidence.snippet: impaired exercise performance is a condition that can be recovered in many people through an individualized physical exercise training program.

evidence.reference_title: Long COVID and rehabilitation.
evidence.snippet: Rehabilitation is a key element of management to achieve functional improvement. Early management should start with comprehensive evaluation and identification of red flags. Exercise-based therapy, an essential part of management of long COVID, can be conducted with different modules, including telerehabilitation.

nodes.description: Difficulty falling asleep or staying asleep.

evidence.reference_title: Characterizing Long COVID: Deep Phenotype of a Complex Condition.
evidence.snippet: Across at least 10 cohorts, authors reported 31 unique clinical features corresponding to HPO terms; the most commonly reported feature was Fatigue (median 45.1%)

nodes.description: Non-invasive light-based intervention studied for post-COVID cognitive dysfunction (brain fog).

evidence.reference_title: Photobiomodulation for cognitive dysfunction (Brain Fog) in post-COVID-19 condition: a randomized double-blind sham-controlled pilot trial.
evidence.snippet: Home-based itPBM is safe and feasible, showing potential cognitive benefits for PCC brain fog, particularly in younger adults.

nodes.description: Heart-rate-lowering pharmacotherapy for Long-COVID patients with orthostatic tachycardia syndromes.
nodes.id: Ivabradine therapy for post-COVID POTS-like tachycardia

evidence.reference_title: Ivabradine effects on COVID-19-associated postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome: a single center prospective study.
evidence.snippet: 43 of 55 (78%) of the included patients reported significant improvement of the symptoms within 7 days of ivabradine therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Oral medications for the treatment of postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome; a systematic review of studies before and during the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence.snippet: Ivabradine, midodrine, and beta-blockers currently appear to be reasonable front-line choices in pharmacologic management of POTS (PASC associated and otherwise).

nodes.description: SARS-CoV-2-associated immune dysregulation may permit reactivation of latent herpesviruses. EBV has the strongest cohort-level support in Long COVID; CMV and VZV remain plausible latent-virus contributors that require biomarker-stratified confirmation.

evidence.reference_title: Viral persistence, reactivation, and mechanisms of long COVID.
evidence.snippet: the role(s) that viral persistence or reactivation of viruses may play in PASC.

evidence.reference_title: Chronic viral coinfections differentially affect the likelihood of developing long COVID.
evidence.snippet: Serological evidence suggesting recent EBV reactivation (early antigen-diffuse IgG positivity) was most strongly associated with fatigue (OR = 2.12).

evidence.reference_title: Lights and Shadows of Long COVID: Are Latent Infections the Real Hidden Enemy?
evidence.snippet: emerging evidence suggests that the reactivation of latent viral infections, such as Epstein-Barr virus, cytomegalovirus, and varicella-zoster virus, may significantly contribute to the complexity of LC.

evidence.reference_title: Multiple early factors anticipate post-acute COVID-19 sequelae.
evidence.snippet: We resolved four PASC-anticipating risk factors at the time of initial COVID-19 diagnosis: type 2 diabetes, SARS-CoV-2 RNAemia, Epstein-Barr virus viremia, and specific auto-antibodies.

evidence.reference_title: Investigation of Long COVID Prevalence and Its Relationship to Epstein-Barr Virus Reactivation.
evidence.snippet: we found that 66.7% (20/30) of long COVID subjects versus 10% (2/20) of control subjects in our primary study group were positive for EBV reactivation based on positive titers for EBV early antigen-diffuse (EA-D) IgG or EBV viral capsid antigen (VCA) IgM.

evidence.reference_title: Distinguishing features of long COVID identified through immune profiling.
evidence.snippet: higher antibody responses directed against non-SARS-CoV-2 viral pathogens were observed among individuals with long COVID, particularly Epstein-Barr virus.

nodes.description: Mast cell activation syndrome (MCAS) has been implicated in Long COVID pathophysiology. Mast cell degranulation releases pro-inflammatory mediators including histamine, contributing to fatigue, brain fog, cardiovascular symptoms, and gastrointestinal disturbances.

evidence.reference_title: Antihistamines improve cardiovascular manifestations and other symptoms of long-COVID attributed to mast cell activation.
evidence.snippet: Previous studies have suggested that mast cell activation (MCA) may play a role in the pathophysiology of long-COVID, including in the mechanisms of its cardiovascular manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Antihistamines improve cardiovascular manifestations and other symptoms of long-COVID attributed to mast cell activation.
evidence.snippet: Long-COVID symptoms disappeared completely in 29% of treated patients. There was a significant improvement in each of the considered symptoms (improved or disappeared) in all treated patients

nodes.description: Mitochondrial impairment drives a shift toward glycolysis with associated oxidative stress and metabolic disturbances, further exacerbating systemic symptoms in Long COVID.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial dysfunction in long COVID: mechanisms, consequences, and potential therapeutic approaches.
evidence.snippet: This review aims to synthesize current findings related to mitochondrial dysfunction in long COVID, exploring its implications for cellular energy deficits, oxidative stress, immune dysregulation, metabolic disturbances, and endothelial dysfunction.

nodes.description: Muscle pain and body aches.

evidence.reference_title: ME/CFS and Long COVID share similar symptoms and biological abnormalities: road map to the literature.
evidence.snippet: They develop persistent fatigue, cognitive problems, headaches, disrupted sleep, myalgias and arthralgias

nodes.description: Sensation of unease in the stomach with urge to vomit.

evidence.reference_title: Characterizing Long COVID: Deep Phenotype of a Complex Condition.
evidence.snippet: the most commonly reported feature was Fatigue (median 45.1%) and the least commonly reported was Nausea (median 3.9%)

nodes.description: Neuroinflammation in Long COVID involves microglial activation, blood-brain barrier disruption, and inflammatory cytokine penetration into the brain parenchyma. This contributes to cognitive dysfunction, memory impairment, and other neurological symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Long Covid brain fog: a neuroinflammation phenomenon?
evidence.snippet: Activation of microglia in response to an immune system challenge can lead to a significant impact on cognitive processes, such as learning, memory and emotional regulation.

evidence.reference_title: Long Covid brain fog: a neuroinflammation phenomenon?
evidence.snippet: Inflammatory cytokines have been found to play a significant role in reductions in LTP and LTD, a reduction in neurogenesis, and in dendritic sprouting.

nodes.description: Excessive heart rate increase upon standing, characteristic of POTS.

evidence.reference_title: ME/CFS and Long COVID share similar symptoms and biological abnormalities: road map to the literature.
evidence.snippet: They develop persistent fatigue, cognitive problems, headaches, disrupted sleep, myalgias and arthralgias, post-exertional malaise, orthostatic intolerance

evidence.reference_title: Autonomic dysfunction in 'long COVID': rationale, physiology and management strategies.
evidence.snippet: It encompasses a plethora of debilitating symptoms (including breathlessness, chest pain, palpitations and orthostatic intolerance)

nodes.description: Awareness of heartbeat, often associated with POTS or general autonomic dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Autonomic dysfunction in 'long COVID': rationale, physiology and management strategies.
evidence.snippet: It encompasses a plethora of debilitating symptoms (including breathlessness, chest pain, palpitations and orthostatic intolerance)

evidence.reference_title: Antihistamines improve cardiovascular manifestations and other symptoms of long-COVID attributed to mast cell activation.
evidence.snippet: Cardiovascular manifestations of long-COVID include high heart rate, postural tachycardia, and palpitations.

nodes.description: Worsening of symptoms following physical or mental exertion, similar to myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: ME/CFS and Long COVID share similar symptoms and biological abnormalities: road map to the literature.
evidence.snippet: They develop persistent fatigue, cognitive problems, headaches, disrupted sleep, myalgias and arthralgias, post-exertional malaise, orthostatic intolerance

evidence.reference_title: ME/CFS and Long COVID share similar symptoms and biological abnormalities: road map to the literature.
evidence.snippet: The illness (Long COVID) is similar to myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS)

nodes.description: Long COVID features reduced peripheral serotonin driven by diminished intestinal tryptophan absorption, platelet hyperactivation that depletes stored serotonin, and enhanced monoamine oxidase-mediated serotonin turnover.

evidence.reference_title: Serotonin reduction in post-acute sequelae of viral infection.
evidence.snippet: Viral infection and type I interferon-driven inflammation reduce serotonin through three mechanisms: diminished intestinal absorption of the serotonin precursor tryptophan; platelet hyperactivation and thrombocytopenia, which impacts serotonin storage; and enhanced MAO-mediated serotonin turnover.

nodes.description: Insomnia, unrefreshing sleep, or altered sleep patterns.

evidence.reference_title: ME/CFS and Long COVID share similar symptoms and biological abnormalities: road map to the literature.
evidence.snippet: They develop persistent fatigue, cognitive problems, headaches, disrupted sleep

nodes.description: Long COVID patients show ongoing systemic inflammation with altered T cell subset distributions and cytokine perturbations, indicating chronic immune activation and dysregulated signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Long COVID manifests with T cell dysregulation, inflammation and an uncoordinated adaptive immune response to SARS-CoV-2.
evidence.snippet: LC individuals exhibited systemic inflammation and immune dysregulation. This was evidenced by global differences in T cell subset distribution implying ongoing immune responses, as well as by sex-specific perturbations in cytolytic subsets.

evidence.reference_title: Distinguishing features of long COVID identified through immune profiling.
evidence.snippet: Marked differences were noted in circulating myeloid and lymphocyte populations relative to the matched controls, as well as evidence of exaggerated humoral responses directed against SARS-CoV-2 among participants with long COVID.

nodes.description: SARS-CoV-2-specific CD8+ T cells exhibit exhaustion phenotypes while CD4+ T cells are poised to migrate to inflamed tissues, reflecting sustained antigen exposure and chronic activation in Long COVID.
nodes.id: T Cell Exhaustion and Tissue Trafficking

evidence.reference_title: Long COVID manifests with T cell dysregulation, inflammation and an uncoordinated adaptive immune response to SARS-CoV-2.
evidence.snippet: LC individuals displayed increased frequencies of CD4+ T cells poised to migrate to inflamed tissues and exhausted SARS-CoV-2-specific CD8+ T cells

nodes.description: Elevated heart rate, often associated with POTS or general autonomic dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Antihistamines improve cardiovascular manifestations and other symptoms of long-COVID attributed to mast cell activation.
evidence.snippet: Cardiovascular manifestations of long-COVID include high heart rate, postural tachycardia, and palpitations.

nodes.description: Peripheral serotonin depletion weakens vagus nerve activity, leading to impaired hippocampal responses and memory formation that contribute to cognitive symptoms in Long COVID.

evidence.reference_title: Serotonin reduction in post-acute sequelae of viral infection.
evidence.snippet: Peripheral serotonin reduction, in turn, impedes the activity of the vagus nerve and thereby impairs hippocampal responses and memory.

nodes.description: SARS-CoV-2 viral reservoirs may persist in tissues including gut, brain, and other organs for months after acute infection. The gastrointestinal tract has emerged as a particularly significant reservoir site, with viral RNA detected in approximately 30% of intestinal specimens from Long COVID patients.

evidence.reference_title: Viral persistence, reactivation, and mechanisms of long COVID.
evidence.snippet: Persistence of SARS-CoV-2 RNA or antigens is reported in some organs, yet the mechanism by which they do so and how they may be associated with pathogenic immune responses is unclear.

evidence.reference_title: Persistent Circulating Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus 2 Spike Is Associated With Post-acute Coronavirus Disease 2019 Sequelae.
evidence.snippet: We detect severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 spike predominantly in PASC patients up to 12 months after diagnosis.
